mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window or positive 20-day returns occur in less than 55% of rate-cut months.

mechanism_narrative: A unemployment rate exceeding 8.0 percent signals severe labor market distress that triggers immediate risk-off positioning and negative equity returns within one week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 5-day returns in more than 60% of observations where UNRATE exceeds 8.0 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates robust aggregate demand that sustains corporate earnings growth and drives positive quarterly equity returns.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in RSAFS and HOUST fails to produce a positive 60-day return in at least 50% of cases over a 5-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: Rebound in the index of industrial production following a sharp contraction confirms supply chain normalization and capacity utilization recovery, yielding positive monthly equity returns.
failure_condition: INDPRO rebounds produce negative 20-day returns in over 55% of post-shock recovery periods, or the signal loses statistical significance after 2020.

mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rates falling below 0.25 percent indicate extreme monetary accommodation that fuels liquidity-driven equity rallies and generates positive weekly returns.
failure_condition: Weekly S&P 500 returns are negative in more than 50% of months where FEDFUNDS remains below 0.25 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising nonfarm payrolls and falling unemployment rates reflects labor market tightening that boosts wage growth and consumer spending, supporting positive quarterly equity performance.
failure_condition: The PAYEMS-UNRATE divergence signal yields negative 60-day returns in at least 55% of observations over a rolling 4-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating real GDP growth during late-cycle expansions signals peak corporate profitability that attracts institutional capital and drives positive monthly equity returns.
failure_condition: GDPC1 acceleration during late-cycle phases produces negative 20-day returns in over 60% of cases, or the predictive power vanishes after 2018.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production dropping below 95.0 percent of trend indicates severe manufacturing contraction that triggers immediate equity sell-offs and negative weekly returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 generates positive 5-day returns in more than 50% of periods where INDPRO falls below the 95.0 threshold.
